Decrease volume for direct discharge from 2020 to 2021.

- different truck used to haul, increasing total VKT
- New trucks had lower haul volume, so had to make more trips
- Very dry weather, reduced precipitation.

- New trucks had lower haul volume, so had to make more trips
- Very dry weather, reduced precipitation.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Commercial production ceased June 30, 2022. The facility was on care and maintenance for all of 2023.
All processing plants are shut down, therefore there are no emissions from process plants, and there is a significant reduction in traffic and road dust generation. The site continues to operate for water management and support services to other Hudbay sites.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Commercial production ceased June 30, 2022. The facility was on care and maintenance for all of 2023.
Significantly reduced traffic and ore haul leading to reduced road dust emissions. The site continues to operate for water management and support services to other Hudbay sites.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Commercial production ceased June 30, 2022. The facility was on care and maintenance for all of 2023.
Significantly reduced traffic and ore haul leading to reduced road dust emissions. The site continues to operate for water management and support services to other Hudbay sites.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
No emissions from process as facility is on care & maintenance.  The site continues to operate for water management and support services to other Hudbay sites.

Point emission releases decreased in 2024 compared to 2023 due to seasonal dryness and decreased need for water management and treatment (lime treatment days were significantly less in 2024).

Increase in road dust is attributed to significantly less precipitation throughout 2024 compared to 2023. Increased generation of dust from vehicular traffic
